name: Bourride of Mahi Mahi with Garlic Mayonnaise
recipeIngredient: 3 Fennel bulbs; stems removed, cores removed, and julienned
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------------Garlic Mayonnaise---------------------------------
1. Peel and finely chop one bulb of garlic.
2. In a bowl, whisk together an egg yolk, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper.
3. Slowly add olive oil while continuously whisking until a mayonnaise consistency is achieved.
4. Add boiling water and stir well. Strain the mixture to remove any impurities before serving.
1. In a small saucepan, bring white wine to a boil. Remove from heat and cool.
2. Stir in saffron and Pernod.
3. Place mahi mahi fillets in the cooled wine mixture and coat with sauce. Let marinate for 2 hours.
1. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add julienned fennel, thyme, tomatoes, salt, and pepper. Sauté for 2-3 minutes or until fennel is tender.
2. Set the sautéed mixture aside and keep warm.
1. In a large saucepan, heat vegetable oil over high heat. Deep-fry reserved fennel stems for 1-2 minutes or until crisp. Drain on paper towels and set aside to keep warm.
1. Grill mahi mahi fillets for 3-4 minutes per side or until cooked through.
2. Serve each fish fillet in the center of a plate, followed by sautéed fennel mixture and drizzled with garlic mayonnaise. Top with deep-fried fennel stems as garnish.
